utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both plans were successfully generated and verified. Note that the output files are large and were truncated in the records, so some detailed content of both plans (particularly specific sections of wo_skills covering non-metallic detection) cannot be fully verified from the truncated output alone.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produced a higher-quality security plan than wo_skills. Both plans passed all verifier checks and covered most evaluation items. However, wi_skills demonstrated a clear advantage in J2-07 (non-metallic weapon detection): it explicitly called out the risk of weapons that evade standard metal detection as a non-negotiable design criterion in the Mission statement, while wo_skills only implied standard screening without naming any specific non-metallic detection technology or policy. Additionally, wi_skills was more precise and structured throughout — using numbered Non-Negotiable Conditions, explicit go/no-go criteria, named threat actors (SAC), and specific room identifiers (AV room 305-A), making it more directly actionable by an executive protection team. The skill-guided plan also showed stronger integration of all threat vectors into a unified security concept, whereas wo_skills, while competent and comprehensive, lacked the same level of operational specificity for non-metallic threats and some specialized protocols emphasized by the skill.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both plans are operationally strong and pass most evaluation criteria, but they differ in one key area: the wo_skills plan explicitly references financial anomalies and psychological/financial screening (confirmed by verifier V3-04 pass), while the wi_skills plan fails V3-04 — omitting explicit psychological and financial re-vetting language for retained high-access personnel. This is a meaningful gap given that R. Caldwell's financial irregularities are the primary insider threat signal. The wo_skills plan also provides a clearer structured threat framework in its Planning Basis section. On other dimensions (timeline phasing, campus blind spots, access reduction, window protection) both plans perform comparably. Overall, wo_skills produces a marginally higher quality output on the critical J3-02 criterion, despite wi_skills having invoked the skill framework. The wi_skills run's failure on V3-04 is a notable deficiency given the scenario's explicit emphasis on financial and psychological screening.
